published_prior_art.cite: Gunsilius2023DiSCo gives weighted Wasserstein/quantile synthetic counterfactuals, not this compatibility-set formulation

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_synth_control_factor_misspec/v1 is scalar Chebyshev misspecification, not distributional rank-factor identification

published_prior_art.cite: Gunsilius2023DiSCo and ArkhangelskyImbens2024PanelSurvey cover DiSCo weighting/averaging, not an augmented affine row-span boundary for rank-invariant quantile factors

published_prior_art.cite: Chen2020DistributionalSC allows poor matching and Gunsilius2023DiSCo gives weighted DSC; neither gives open-set exact-prefit nonidentification from augmented affine escape
Gunsilius2023DiSCo: weighted donor quantile curve in the stable convex-loading corner

novelty_flags.label: proposal
novelty_flags.one_line: The drafter did not emit the required named literature checklist mapping 4-10 candidate papers to §8 statements; I reconstructed from §5, but the handoff itself is absent.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 1 / Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The setup says unit loadings b_i are fixed across ranks, but \mathcal{B}(P,u) and \mathcal{Q}_I(P) allow rank-by-rank compatible tuples, so the identified set may include laws with u-varying loadings unless a global cross-rank compatibility set is defined.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The 'donor-weight submodel' used for the row-span equivalence is not formally defined as an assumption or model class, so the iff boundary currently mixes the general quantile-factor law with an implicit affine-weight restriction.

soundness_flags.label: Locked parameters / Assumptions
soundness_flags.one_line: The locked block says 'finite-support donor/pre-period observability' while the formal setup uses general quantile curves; replace with 'finite-donor/pre-period observability' or add an actual finite-support restriction.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace \mathcal{B}(P,u) with a global compatibility object \mathcal{B}(P) carrying one common set of unit loadings b_i across all ranks, then define admissible q as selections from that global object.
Add a formal definition of the donor-weight submodel before §8 and state exactly which extra restrictions make z(u) in rowspan \bar X(u) equivalent to post-period functional invariance.

Add the missing literature checklist table with per-result mappings for Gunsilius2023DiSCo, ArkhangelskyImbens2024PanelSurvey, Chen2020DistributionalSC, Masini2025DCA, and ChenFeng2023GroupHeterogeneousCICDSC.
For Conjecture 2, add a small explicit nondegenerate J=3, T0=1 or T0=2 rank-panel schematic showing the affine null direction and two monotone post curves before relying on the open-set promotion.

recommended_next_step: Revise the compatibility definitions to enforce cross-rank fixed loadings, define the donor-weight submodel, and add the missing checklist before another novelty pass.
